pr.title: Stage splitting of text_3 upper and text_2 lower
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Split several stage assembly ranges out of the monolithic asm/text_3.s upper region and split grfzerocar out of the lower end of asm/text_2.s, creating standalone stage asm files and wiring them into the build/link order. The PR was a large mechanical assembly reorganization rather than C decompilation, reducing reliance on huge text_N blobs and making individual stage modules addressable for future work.
what_changed: Removed 850 lines from asm/text_2.s at its lower end and added them as the new standalone asm/grfzerocar.s object.
Reduced asm/text_3.s from a large 11925-line stage-containing blob to a minimal 9-line stub in the diff, moving its upper stage ranges into standalone stage assembly files.
Added new stage asm files: asm/grbattle.s, asm/grfigureget.s, asm/grflatzone.s, asm/grgreens.s, asm/grlast.s, asm/grpura.s, asm/grpushon.s, and asm/grfzerocar.s.

Updated ldscript.lcf FORCEFILES to include grfzerocar.o near the text_2 split point and to include grpura.o, grgreens.o, grflatzone.o, grpushon.o, grfigureget.o, grbattle.o, and grlast.o before the remaining text_3.o.
Updated obj_files.mk TEXT_O_FILES with the same newly split asm objects so the build produces and links the new stage object files.
smart_moves: Kept the split mechanical: the new files preserve raw assembly, existing labels, global symbols, address comments, and .section declarations, minimizing risk while changing object boundaries.
Mirrored the linker-script ordering in obj_files.mk, which is essential for assembly splits where object order preserves the final DOL layout.
Separated stage-specific code into gr*.s files instead of leaving it embedded in text_2.s/text_3.s, making later stage decompilation easier to scope and search.
Placed grfzerocar.o immediately after text_2.o in the object list/linker order, matching the PR description that it came from the lower end of text_2.
Placed the other newly split stage objects immediately before text_3.o, matching the PR description that they came from the upper end of text_3.
decomp_lessons: For early decomp cleanup, splitting monolithic text_N assembly blobs into named subsystem or stage objects can be valuable even before converting code to C.

When splitting assembly, update both the makefile object list and linker script in lockstep; missing either side can build the object but link it incorrectly, or list it in the linker without producing it.
Preserve object order exactly around split boundaries. In this PR, grfzerocar was inserted after text_2.o, while the text_3-derived stage objects were inserted before text_3.o.
Use stage file prefixes consistently: stage assembly modules were named grbattle.s, grfigureget.s, grflatzone.s, grfzerocar.s, grgreens.s, grlast.s, grpura.s, and grpushon.s.

A successful assembly split can be mostly deletion from a monolithic blob plus identical additions to smaller named files; the important correctness signal is link/build ordering, not semantic rewrites.
naming_conventions: Stage assembly files use the gr*.s prefix matching existing stage object naming, e.g. grbattle.s, grgreens.s, grlast.s, grfzerocar.s.
Object names in ldscript.lcf mirror the asm file basename with .o, e.g. asm/grgreens.s becomes grgreens.o.
Object names in obj_files.mk mirror the asm path under $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/, e.g. $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/grgreens.o.
No new decompiled C symbol names were introduced; functions and labels remained placeholder names such as func_80219C98 and lbl_804D6AC8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used standalone .s files with .include "macros.inc" and .section .text declarations to preserve assembler context after splitting.
Preserved .global declarations and raw labels from the original text blobs, avoiding symbol renaming during the split.
Maintained address comments in the assembly, which helps verify that copied ranges still correspond to the expected original addresses.
Kept FORCEFILES ordering aligned with TEXT_O_FILES ordering to maintain the binary layout after moving contiguous assembly ranges into separate objects.
The diff indicates a range-split tactic: delete contiguous blocks from asm/text_2.s and asm/text_3.s and re-add them as full new gr*.s files.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
There is no explicit reviewer rationale in the evidence beyond the PR title/body and the mechanical diff.
searchable_terms: Stage splitting of text_3 upper and text_2 lower
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or further split grbattle, grgreens, grlast, grpura, grpushon, grflatzone, grfigureget, or grfzerocar.
Check whether the object ordering introduced in PR 36 remained stable in later ldscript.lcf and obj_files.mk changes.
Search for later renames of placeholder symbols from the newly split gr*.s files into stage-specific C functions.
Compare adjacent stage split PRs to confirm the project convention for placing split objects around text_2.o and text_3.o.
